question_text:
Intersection of Two Linked Lists

Given the heads of two singly linked-lists `headA` and `headB`, return the node at which the two lists intersect. If the two linked lists have no intersection at all, return `null`.
input_format: Space-separated values as per problem arguments.
output_format: A single line representing the result.
explanation: We use two pointers. Each pointer traverses its list and then the other list. This way, both pointers travel the same total distance `(a+c+b)` and `(b+c+a)` before meeting at the intersection point `c`. O(M+N) time and O(1) space.
